methods.description: Process for collecting sediment samples from Antarctic lakes to study geological and biological characteristics.

steps.description: Sediments were collected from Skua Lake and Soto Lake during the austral summer of 2016/17 and from Katerina Lake and Florencia Lake in the austral summer of 2020/21.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment samples from specified Antarctic lakes

steps.description: Samples from all lakes were collected in triplicate using PVC pipes (60 mm diameter × 50 mm length), disinfected to avoid contamination.
steps.searchable_summary: collect triplicate sediment samples using disinfected PVC pipes

steps.description: Sediments were sampled from three different points (each separated by approximately 50 m) in the littoral region of each lake at a depth of 20-50 cm.
steps.searchable_summary: sample sediments from three points in littoral region at specified depth

steps.description: Approximately 500 g of each sediment sample was immediately sub-sampled, sealed, placed in sterile Whirl-pack bags and frozen at −20 °C until processing.
steps.searchable_summary: sub-sample, seal, and freeze sediment samples

methods.description: Chemical analysis of sediment samples to determine pH, potential acidity, exchangeable cations, and total organic carbon.

steps.description: pH was determined using a 1:2.5 sediment:deionized water ratio.
steps.searchable_summary: determine pH of sediment using water ratio

steps.description: Potential acidity (H + Al) was extracted with 0.5 mol L−1 Ca(OAc)2 buffered to pH 7.0 and quantified by titration with 0.0606 mol L−1 NaOH.
steps.searchable_summary: extract and quantify potential acidity by titration

steps.description: Exchangeable Ca2+, Mg2+ and Al3+ were extracted with 1 mol L−1 KCl, and K+ and P+ were extracted with Melich 16.
steps.searchable_summary: extract exchangeable cations using KCl and Melich 16

steps.description: Element levels in the extracts were determined by ICP (Al3+), flame emission (Na+, K+) and photocolorimetry (P) by the ascorbic acid method.
steps.searchable_summary: determine element levels using ICP, flame emission, and photocolorimetry

steps.description: Total organic carbon (TOC) was quantified by wet oxidation using the Walkley-Black method.
steps.searchable_summary: quantify TOC using Walkley-Black method

steps.description: Total cation exchange capacity (CEC) was calculated as the sum of the bases (Ca2+, Mg2+, K+, Al3+) and potential acidity (H+ + Al3+).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate CEC from base and acidity values

methods.description: Extraction of DNA from sediment samples and preparation for sequencing to identify fungal species.

steps.description: Three replicate sub-samples were taken from the center of each core section under strict contamination control conditions.
steps.searchable_summary: take replicate sub-samples under contamination control

steps.description: Total DNA was extracted using the FastDNA Spin Kit for Soil, following the manufacturer's instructions.
steps.searchable_summary: extract DNA using FastDNA Spin Kit

steps.description: DNA quality was analyzed by agarose gel electrophoresis and quantified using the Quanti-iT™ Pico Green dsDNA Assay.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze and quantify DNA quality

steps.description: The ITS2 of the nuclear ribosomal DNA was used as a DNA barcode for molecular species identification using universal primers ITS3 and ITS4.
steps.searchable_summary: use ITS2 as DNA barcode for species identification

steps.description: Sequences were imported to QIIME2 for bioinformatics analyses using the qiime2-dada2 plugin for filtering, dereplication, merging, and chimera removal.
steps.searchable_summary: perform bioinformatics analysis using QIIME2

steps.description: Taxonomic assignments were determined for ASVs using the qiime2-feature-classifier against the UNITE Eukaryotes ITS database.
steps.searchable_summary: assign taxonomy to ASVs using QIIME2

methods.description: Analysis of fungal diversity and ecological roles in sediment samples using various indices and functional assignments.

steps.description: Relative abundances of ASVs were used to quantify fungal taxa, with categories for dominant, intermediate, and minor components.
steps.searchable_summary: quantify fungal taxa using ASV relative abundances

steps.description: Taxon diversity, richness, and dominance were quantified using Fisher's α, Margalef's, and Simpson's indices.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate diversity indices for fungal taxa

steps.description: Species accumulation curves were obtained using the Mao Tao index.
steps.searchable_summary: generate species accumulation curves

steps.description: Functional assignments of fungal ASVs were prepared using FunGuild.
steps.searchable_summary: assign functional roles to fungal ASVs
